Import the necessary modules from Keras and TensorFlow Datasets.
Define a batch size of 32 and image dimensions of 299x299.
Load the Oxford Flowers dataset using `tensorflow_datasets.load` with split='train'.
Preprocess the input images to resize them to (299, 299) and normalize pixel values.
Split the preprocessed dataset into training and validation sets with a ratio of 80:20.
Compile the model with the 'categorical_crossentropy' loss function, 'adam' optimizer, and accuracy metric.
Apply CutMix, MixUp, and RandAugment augmentations to the training dataset using custom pipeline functions.
Train the model on the augmented training dataset for 1 epoch.
Validate the model performance on the validation dataset.
Check if the validation loss contains NaN values after the first epoch.
